title: Pet Age Calculator: Human Years | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Calculate Your Pet's Age in Human Years
description: Convert your dog or cat's age to human years with our free calculator. Adjust for breed and size. Updated 2026.
intro: Responsible pet care requires understanding your dog's or cat's species, breed, and life stage-specific needs. Proper nutrition, vaccinations, and exercise form the foundation of good health. This calculator converts your pet's age to human years based on size and breed, accounting for how different breeds age at different rates. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **logarithmic calculation**. Direct application with your inputs. Get your dog's or cat's age in human years based on breed and size. Updated for 2026.
useCases: Determine your pet's life stage for nutrition and care decisions
Pet owners planning appropriate exercise routines by age
Veterinary professionals validating patient age conversions
Understanding when dogs transition to senior status by breed
Quick reference before veterinary visits or health decisions
explanation:
**Pet care** follows international veterinary guidelines from organizations like WSAVA and AAHA. Feeding schedules, vaccination protocols, and parasite prevention are established by species, breed, weight, and age.

## How It's Calculated

Different dog breeds and sizes age at different rates. A dog's first year equals roughly 15 human years, the second year adds 9 human years, and subsequent years vary based on breed and size. Larger dogs age faster and tend to have shorter lifespans, while smaller breeds age more slowly and often live longer. Cats follow a different curve, with slower initial aging compared to dogs.

This calculator applies a logarithmic formula adjusted for your pet's type, size, and age, flagging any unreasonable inputs.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a helpful reference for understanding your pet's development. For critical decisions affecting your pet's health, always consult a veterinary professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How do I calculate my dog's age in human years?
faq.a: A dog's first year equals about 15 human years, the second year adds 9 human years, and subsequent years depend on the dog's size and breed.

faq.q: Why do large dogs age faster than small dogs?
faq.a: Large and giant breeds age more rapidly and have shorter lifespans. For example, a 10-year-old large dog is roughly 78 human years old, while a small dog at 10 years is around 56 human years.

faq.q: At what age is a dog considered senior?
faq.a: Senior status depends on breed size: giant breeds at 6+, large breeds at 7-8, medium breeds at 8+, and small breeds at 10+ years.

faq.q: How does a cat's age compare to a dog's age?
faq.a: Cats age more slowly than dogs initially. A 5-year-old cat is approximately 36 human years old, a different progression than similarly-aged dogs.

faq.q: Do different dog breeds have different life expectancies?
faq.a: Yes, significantly. A Chihuahua may live 15+ years while a Great Dane typically lives 7-10 years. Size is the biggest factor in canine lifespan.

faq.q: Are mixed-breed dogs healthier than purebreds?
faq.a: Mixed-breed dogs generally tend to be more long-lived than purebreds, often due to greater genetic diversity and fewer inherited health issues.

faq.q: Is this calculator accurate for mixed-breed dogs?
faq.a: For mixed breeds, use the size category that best matches your dog's current weight. The calculation provides a reasonable estimate, though individual variation exists.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all of our pet calculators and tools are completely free and require no account registration.
